raw:
**SCENE ANALYSIS - A wooden cutting board is on a table, with a single onion placed on it. The onion is a light brown, round vegetable. STRATEGY - First, grasp the onion to pick it up. Then, transport it to a suitable chopping area (implied to be the cutting board itself). Next, perform the chopping action. Finally, place the chopped onion back onto the cutting board. Failure modes include dropping the onion during transport or failing to chop it properly. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1: Reach for the onion on the cutting board. Step 2: Grasp the onion to pick it up. Step 3: Transport the onion to the chopping area. Step 4: Chop the onion. Step 5: Place the chopped onion back on the cutting board.**
Goal: chopped(onion); on(onion, cutting_board)
Scene: onion, cutting_board, table
Step 1: reach(onion)
  affordance_region: the body of the onion
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, onion, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, onion) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(onion)
  affordance_region: the body of the onion
  contact: holding(onion) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(onion)
Step 3: transport(onion -> cutting_board)
  affordance_region: an empty area on the cutting board
  spatial: above(onion, cutting_board, 0.05) [completion]
  safety: no_drop(onion)
  done_when: above(onion, cutting_board, 0.05)
Step 4: chop(onion)
  affordance_region: the body of the onion
  contact: holding(onion) [progress]
  pose: stable(onion) [progress]
  done_when: chopped(onion)
Step 5: place(onion -> cutting_board)
  affordance_region: an empty area on the cutting board
  contact: surface_contact(onion, cutting_board) [completion]; released(onion) [completion]
  pose: stable(onion) [completion]
  done_when: surface_contact(onion, cutting_board) AND released(onion)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: an empty area on the cutting board
an empty area on the cutting board
